revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:平群坐紀氏神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = {{ill|Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|ja|平群坐紀氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|qq=}} | image = File:Kishi-jinja, haiden.jpg | location = {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan | coordinates = {{Coord|34.635653|N|135.703617|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|qid=Q17216122|1=Heguri no Tsuku}} | type = {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}})
Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | established = Unknown | main_building = {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | annual_festival = First Sunday of October | alternate_name = {{ill|Kishi Shrine|ja|紀氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | map_type = Japan Nara | prefecture= {{ill|Heguri|qid=Q1346790|1=Heguri, Nara}} | architecture_style= {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} }} [[File:Kishi-jinja, torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] {{ill|Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|ja|平群坐紀氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine|qq=}} (へぐりにますきしじんじゃ / へぐりにますきのうじじんじゃ / へぐりにいますきうじのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Ikoma District, Nara|en|Ikoma District, Nara|qid=Q971003|lt=Ikoma District, Nara|1=Ikoma District, Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinai shrine|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinai shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki_Jinmyocho}} ({{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) and was formerly classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. The shrine is also referred to simply as {{ill|Kishi Shrine|ja|紀氏神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Enshrined Deities == The shrine currently enshrines the following four deities: * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|qid=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tuku|1=Heguri no Tsuku}} (Heguri no Tuku Sukune) * {{ill|Hachiman|en|Hachiman|qid=Q261637|lt=Hachiman|1=Hachiman}} The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} as "Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine" (平群坐紀氏神社), meaning "The Shrine of the Kishi Clan residing in Heguri." The enshrined deity was originally the ancestral deity of the {{ill|Kishi clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, a lineage of {{ill|Kuge|en|Kuge|qid=Q1196276|lt=Kuge|1=Kuge}} (court nobility). The Kishi clan consisted of two distinct lineages: the {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}, who governed {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|qid=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}}, and the central aristocratic Kishi clan (皇別紀氏), who played a role in the imperial court. The shrine was established by the latter. The exact relationship between the two lineages is unclear, but records indicate that the Kishi clan had a strong presence in the Heguri area. This is supported by the presence of the nearby {{ill|Sanri Kofun|ja|三里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanri Kofun|1=Misato Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60990385}}, which features a stone chamber similar to those found in {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|qid=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}, the homeland of the Kii lineage. A notable figure enshrined at the shrine is {{ill|Heguri no Tsuku|en|Heguri no Tsuku|qid=Q17216122|lt=Heguri no Tuku|1=Heguri no Tsuku}}, who was the elder brother of {{ill|Ki no Sumi|ja|紀角|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Sumi|1=Ki no Tsuno|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605107}}. A similarly named shrine, {{ill|Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|ja|平群神社 (桑名市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heguri Shrine (Kuwana)|qq=}}, located in {{ill|Kuwana, Mie|en|Kuwana, Mie|qid=Q496472|lt=Kuwana, Mie|1=Kuwana, Mie}}, also enshrines Heguri no Tuku. == History == The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. A reference to the "Kishi deity" appears in the {{ill|Ruijū Kokushi|en|Ruijū Kokushi|qid=Q1038639|lt=Ruijū Kokushi|1=Ruijū Kokushi}}, stating that in 824 CE (Tenchō 1), {{ill|Ki no Momotsugu|ja|紀百継|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Momotsugu|1=Ki no Momotsugu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605090}} and {{ill|Ki no Suetsugu|ja|紀末成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Suetsugu|1=Ki no Suenari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11605070}} petitioned the court for the shrine to receive imperial offerings. Another mention appears in the {{ill|Nihon Kiryaku|en|Nihon Kiryaku|qid=Q1053542|lt=Nihon Kiryaku|1=Nihon Kiryaku}}, where in 829 CE (Tenchō 6), land in {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Atago District, Kyoto|ja|愛宕郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atago District, Kyoto|1=Otagi district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10981345}}, was granted to Ki no Momotsugu as a sacred site for the shrine. A document from 870 CE (Jōgan 12) records a land sale in "Heguri Higashi-jō, Heguri-ri," with the names of two Kishi clan members—Ki no Seyo and Ki no Motoo—listed as guarantors. It also states that the "Kishi Shrine Land" was located south of this area, suggesting that the shrine was originally located about 2.5 km south of its current location, in the {{ill|Tsubai area|ja|椿井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai area|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Heguri. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, compiled in 927 CE (Engi 5), lists Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine as a {{ill|Myōjin Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin Taisha|comment=enwiki is a redirect|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, which received imperial offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-sai|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-sai|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}. The shrine is also mentioned in the section on {{ill|Myōjin-sai|ja|名神祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-sai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 名神大社|1=Myōjin Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q9610964}}, which designated it as one of the shrines to receive special offerings from the imperial court. During the Edo period, the shrine was popularly known as "Tsuji no Miya" (辻の宮) or "Tsubaki no Miya" (椿の宮). An early Edo-period stone lantern at the shrine bears the inscription "Kasuga Daimyōjin" (春日大明神), indicating syncretism with {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|en|Kasuga-taisha|qid=Q714559|lt=Kasuga-taisha|1=Kasuga-taisha}}. After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine was classified as a {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} in 1873 under the Modern Shrine Ranking System. == Shrine Grounds == The main shrine building is constructed in {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|qid=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} style, facing west. It is painted vermilion and has a copper roof. The building underwent restoration in June 2012. In front of the main shrine is the haiden (worship hall), with three auxiliary buildings known as "Za-goya" (座小屋). These buildings are positioned on the north, west, and south sides of the haiden and correspond to the three local districts of Kamishō, Shijihara, and Nishimukai. The northern and southern Za-goya have earthen floors, while the western Za-goya has wooden flooring, reflecting its historical use for receiving officials during the medieval period. The shrine also enshrines {{ill|Kasuga Shrines|lt=Kasuga Shrine (境内社)|ja|春日神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrines|qq=}}, which was merged with another Kasuga Shrine from the Nishimoto area in 1910. File:Kishi-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine (honden) File:Kishi-jinja, keidai.jpg|Haiden and Za-goya File:Kishi-jinja, keidaisha.jpg|Auxiliary shrine (境内社) == Access == '''Address''' * {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|qid=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, Ikoma District, Nara Prefecture, Japan '''Transportation''' * **Rail**: {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|qid=Q900218|lt=Kintetsu Ikoma Line|1=Ikoma Line}} - **Motoyama-guchi Station** (approx. 20-minute walk) '''Nearby Sites''' * {{ill|Prince Nagaya|en|Prince Nagaya|qid=Q1963492|lt=Nagaya no Ō|1=Prince Nagaya}} Tomb * {{ill|Kibi Naishinnō|ja|吉備内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Naishinnō|1=Kibi-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11412639}} Tomb * {{ill|Sanri Kofun|ja|三里古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanri Kofun|1=Misato Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q60990385}} – Nara Prefecture-designated Historic Site. == References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kishi clan|ja|紀氏|1=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|de|Ki (Klan)|uk|Рід Кі|ko|기씨 (황별)|fr|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|3=紀伊国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kishi clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/kindex2.php?J_ID=26001 Heguri-nimasu Kishi Shrine] – Kokugakuin University {{Hachiman Faith}} {{translated page|ja|平群坐紀氏神社|version=103083108|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:平群坐紀氏神社]] {{Commons category|Kishi-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11483495}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:History of Heguri, Nara]] [[Category:Ki clan]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Son-sha in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]]
